mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) reflects strengthening consumer demand, which raises expected corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of months during any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a trailing three-month window signals labor market deterioration, triggering recession expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in over 55 percent of triggered instances during any 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated levels of the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above five percent and high consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above three hundred index points create a restrictive financial environment that jointly compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds five percent and CPIAUCSL exceeds three hundred, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than half of the concurrent months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In the immediate aftermath of a recession, month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) signal the restoration of manufacturing capacity and a rebound in operating leverage, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO rebounded sharply after 2020 recession alongside positive SPX returns
failure_condition: During quarters officially classified as within two years of a business-cycle trough, month-over-month INDPRO increases coincide with negative S&P 500 5-day returns in over 50 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the effective federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals an easing monetary stance that lowers the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS month-over-month declines are followed by negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1,400 thousand units indicates a contraction in residential investment and a weakening household balance-sheet channel, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1,400 thousand units, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in over 55 percent of months during any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and real retail sales (RSAFS) reinforce the income-consumption loop, confirming broad-based demand strength and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases month-over-month and RSAFS increases month-over-month
failure_condition: In months when both PAYEMS and RSAFS increase month-over-month, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of those joint-up months over any 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding one percent signals deteriorating manufacturing momentum and negative operating leverage, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines by more than one percent over three months, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in over 55 percent of triggered months over any 5-year rolling window.
